description:
You are acting as the company’s backup-administrator.  
    An internal HTML “daily report” is already present on disk and must be harvested with a headless browser, then archived.

    Environment that already exists BEFORE you start  
      • The directory /home/user/mock_site/ contains a single HTML file named data.html.  
      • The directory /home/user/backup/ exists and is writable by you.  

    Goal  
      1. Serve the file /home/user/mock_site/data.html locally on TCP port 8000 (any lightweight, head-less technique such as Python’s built-in http.server is fine).  
      2. Using a totally head-less browser tool (curl, wget, etc.), download the page from  
         http://localhost:8000/data.html  
      3. Extract ONLY the lines that contain the literal text “ARCHIVE:” (case-sensitive).  
         – Strip every HTML tag so that only the bare text remains.  
         – Preserve the exact order in which the “ARCHIVE:” lines appear in the source file.  
      4. Save the cleaned result to /home/user/backup/20230804_scraped_archives.log

         Output format that **will be auto-checked**:
             • The file must contain exactly two lines.  
             • Each line must end with a single trailing newline <LF>.  
             • The lines must be exactly:
                   ARCHIVE: db_full_2023-08-04.tar.gz
                   ARCHIVE: logs_2023-08-04.tar.gz

      5. Pack the log file into a gzip-compressed tarball called
            /home/user/backup/archive_20230804.tgz
         The tarball must contain exactly one file at its root:
            20230804_scraped_archives.log

    Success criteria that automated tests will verify  
      • /home/user/backup/20230804_scraped_archives.log exists and matches the two-line format above precisely (including newlines, no extra spaces).  
      • /home/user/backup/archive_20230804.tgz exists, is a valid gzip-compressed tar archive, and tar -tzf shows exactly:  
            20230804_scraped_archives.log  
      • The content of the file inside the tarball is byte-for-byte identical to /home/user/backup/20230804_scraped_archives.log.

    Hints (do NOT output the commands themselves)  
      • A simple “python3 -m http.server 8000” launched in the mock_site directory is sufficient to serve the file.  
      • grep / sed / cut / awk are all acceptable for stripping tags and keeping only the ARCHIVE lines.  
      • Remember to background the web-server (or run it in a separate shell) so that your scraping command can execute.  
      • A two- to three-command solution is perfectly adequate (start server, download & filter, create tar.gz).
truth:
Pre-existing file BEFORE task starts Path: /home/user/mock_site/data.html Contents (each item on its own line, ending in ):

Daily Backup Report

ARCHIVE: db_full_2023-08-04.tar.gz

ARCHIVE: logs_2023-08-04.tar.gz

STATUS: Completed

Files the agent must create 1. /home/user/backup/20230804_scraped_archives.log Exact contents, byte-for-byte (each line ends with a single , no trailing blank lines): ARCHIVE: db_full_2023-08-04.tar.gz\n ARCHIVE: logs_2023-08-04.tar.gz\n 2. /home/user/backup/archive_20230804.tgz – Valid gzip-compressed tar archive. – tar -tzf /home/user/backup/archive_20230804.tgz prints exactly: 20230804_scraped_archives.log – Extracting that file reproduces the exact byte sequence shown above.
